description_long: A standard egg carton made of molded gray pulp holds a dozen white eggs arranged in two rows of six. The eggs are smooth, oval, and uniformly white, each marked with faint red printed text on the upper surface. The carton features individual rounded compartments for each egg, with raised dividers between them to prevent movement and breakage. The material of the carton appears slightly textured and matte, contrasting with the glossy, hard shells of the eggs. The overall form is rectangular, with the eggs sitting snugly in their slots. The object is compact and lightweight, suitable for easy handling and transport.
description: A gray pulp egg carton holds twelve smooth, white eggs with faint red markings, arranged in two neat rows.
description_view_0: Front view showing all twelve eggs in two rows, with red markings visible on the eggs' upper surfaces.
description_view_1: End view displaying the rounded tops of the eggs and the lengthwise arrangement of the carton.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the eggs and carton from the opposite side, with red markings still visible.
description_view_3: Opposite end view, again highlighting the rounded tops of the eggs and the carton’s length.
attribute_orientation: lying flat with eggs upright in individual compartments
attribute_contextual_placement: Most commonly found in a kitchen or grocery store, used for storing and transporting eggs safely.
attribute_decorative_patterns: faint red printed text on eggs
